title: Geometric Modeling for Mechanical Design
description: Foundations of geometric modeling as applied to mechanical design through use of modern computer-aided design (CAD) and physical prototyping tools; basics of systematic design methodology; geometric visualization concepts: multiview orthographic, isometric, oblique, perspective; three-dimensional representations, surface and solid modeling; dimensioning and tolerancing; rapid prototyping using 3D printing.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Mechanical engineering major; grade of C or better in ENGR 102 .

title: Statics and Particle Dynamics
description: Application of the fundamental principles of Newtonian mechanics to the statics and dynamics of particles; equilibrium of trusses, frames, beams and other rigid bodies.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: For non-mechanical engineering majors; admission to an engineering major; Grade of C or better in PHYS 206 or PHYS 218; grade of C or better in MATH 251 or MATH 253 , or concurrent enrollment.

title: Materials Science
description: Mechanical, optical, thermal, magnetic and electrical properties of solids; differences in properties of metals, polymers, ceramics and composite materials in terms of bonding and crystal structure.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in CHEM 107 or CHEM 119 ; grade of C or better in PHYS 206 . Cross Listing: MSEN 222/MEEN 222 .

title: Principles of Materials and Manufacturing
description: Structures of metals, polymers and ceramics, including structure-mechanical property relationships; defects and diffusion in materials; basic machining theory and processes, including geometric dimensioning and tolerancing (GD&T); overview of manufacturing processes for metals and polymers, including additive technologies.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in CHEM 120 , or CHEM 107 and CHEM 117 ; grade of C or better in PHYS 206 .

title: Engineering Mechanics
description: Application of the laws of classical mechanics to simplified, plausibly real world problems or interest to mechanical engineering, including the analysis of cables, frames, trusses, beams, machines and mechanisms.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Mechanical engineering major; grade of C or better in PHYS 206 ; grade of C or better in MATH 251 or MATH 253 , or concurrent enrollment.

title: Mechanical Measurements
description: Introduction to the basic principles of engineering experimentation including: instrumentation and measurement techniques, signal processing and data acquisition, statistical data analysis, and interpretation and reporting of results.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in STAT 211 ; Grade of C or better in ECEN 215 , MATH 308 and MEEN 315 , or concurrent enrollment.

title: Directed Studies
description: Directed studies in specific problem areas of mechanical engineering.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: MEEN classification; approval by instructor and department head or delegate.

title: Special Topics in...
description: Selected topics in an identified area of mechanical engineering. May be repeated for credit.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Approval of instructor.

title: Research
description: Research conducted under the direction of faculty member in mechanical engineering. May be repeated 2 times for credit.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Freshman or sophomore classification and approval of instructor.
repeatability: May be repeated 2 times for credit.

title: Solid Mechanics
description: Applications of stress and deformation relationships for deformable bodies and mechanical elements relevant to mechanical engineers; to include axially loaded members, stability of columns, torsional members and beams, failure theories, combined loadings; introduction to structural design.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in MEEN 225 ; grade of C or better in MEEN 210 or concurrent enrollment.

title: Principles of Thermodynamics
description: Theory and application of energy methods in engineering; conservation of mass and energy; energy transfer by heat, work and mass; thermodynamic properties; analysis of open and closed systems; the second law of thermodynamics and entropy; gas, vapor and refrigeration cycles.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of a C or better in MEEN 221 or MEEN 225 ; grade of a C or better in MATH 251 or MATH 253 .

title: Fluid Mechanics
description: Application of laws of statics, buoyancy, stability, energy and momentum to behavior of ideal and real fluids; dimensional analysis and similitude and their application to flow through ducts and piping; lift and drag and related problems.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in MEEN 315 and MATH 308 .

title: Fluid Mechanics Laboratory
description: Introduction to basic fluid mechanics instrumentation; experimental verification and reinforcement of the analytical concepts introduced in MEEN 344 .
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in MEEN 260 ; grade of C or better in MEEN 344 or concurrent enrollment.

title: Engineering Analysis for Mechanical Engineers
description: Practical foundation for the use of numerical methods to solve engineering problems; error estimation, Taylor series, numerical solution of linear and non-linear algebraic and differential equations; introduction to engineering optimization.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in MATH 308 .

title: Materials and Manufacturing Selection in Design
description: Selecting materials and manufacturing processes in design; emphasis on mechanical properties of materials; microstructure production and control; manufacturing processes for producing various classes of materials.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in MEEN 223 and MEEN 260 .

title: Materials and Manufacturing in Design Laboratory
description: Experiments in materials characterization and manufacturing processes; emphasis on material mechanical properties; microstructure production and control; manufacturing processes for producing various shapes for components and structures.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in MEEN 210 and MEEN 260 ; grade of C or better in MEEN 360 or concurrent enrollment.

title: Dynamics and Vibrations
description: Application of Newtonian and energy methods to model dynamic systems (particles and rigid bodies) with ordinary differential equations; solution of models using analytical and numerical approaches; interpreting solutions; linear vibrations.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in MEEN 225 or MEEN 221 , and MATH 308 ; grade of a C or better in MEEN 357 and MEEN 305 , or concurrent enrollment.

title: Dynamic Systems and Controls
description: Mathematical modeling and analysis of different types of dynamic systems; introduction to feedback control, time and frequency domain analysis of control systems, stability, PID control, root locus; design of computer-based controllers.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in MEEN 260 , MEEN 363 , and ECEN 215 .

title: Dynamic Systems and Controls Lab
description: Introduction to basic control systems instrumentation; experimental verification of control system concepts; implementation of computer-based controllers; data acquisition and analysis.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in MEEN 260 , MEEN 363 , and ECEN 215 ; grade of C or better in MEEN 364 , or concurrent enrollment.

title: Solid Mechanics in Mechanical Design
description: Design of structural/mechanical members for stiffness, strength and stability under a variety of loading conditions; use of Static and Fatigue Failure Theories to estimate life of components.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of a C or better in MEEN 305 ; grade of C or better in MEEN 210 , or concurrent enrollment.

title: Seminar
description: Presentations by practicing engineers and faculty addressing: effective communications, engineering practices, professional registration, ethics, career-long competence, contemporary issues, impact of technology on society and being informed; preparation of a resume, a lifelong learning plan, two papers, two oral presentations and complete an online assessment of the mechanical engineering program.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Major in mechanical engineering.

title: High Impact Experience for Mechanical Engineers
description: Participation in an approved high-impact learning practice; reflection on professional outcomes from engineering body of knowledge; documentation and self-assessment of learning experience at mid-curriculum point.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Junior or senior classification.

title: Mechanical Engineering Design I
description: The design innovation process; need definition, functional analysis, performance requirements and evaluation criteria, conceptual design evaluation, down-selected to an embodiment; introduction to systems and concurrent engineering; parametric and risk analysis, failure mode analysis, material selection, and manufacturability; cost and life cycle issues, project management.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in MEEN 360 , MEEN 361 and MEEN 368 ; grade of C or better in MEEN 364 , MEEN 365 , MEEN 461 and MEEN 464 , or concurrent enrollment.

title: Intermediate Design
description: Product detail design and development process including case studies; project management, marketing considerations, manufacturing, detailed design specifications; failure modes, application of codes and standards, selection of design margins; product (component) development guidelines; intellectual property, product liability and ethical responsibility.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in MEEN 401 .

title: Experimental Design and Data Analysis
description: This course covers the basics of design, planning and execution of engineering experiments, as well as statistical and data analysis methods. The course includes a hands-on engineering project in a group setting, consisting of a literature review, hypothesis and objective formulation, experimentation, statistical testing, data visualization and the reporting and presentation of results.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in MEEN 345 and MEEN 361 .

title: Engineering Vibrations
description: Principles of dynamic system modeling with emphasis on second order mechanical systems. Harmonic and nonharmonic vibrations of single and multi-degree of freedom systems. Impulse response function, response to arbitrary functions and periodic inputs. Vibration absorption and tuned mass dampers. Applications of computer simulation and analysis techniques in vibrations.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in MEEN 363 .

title: Energy Management in Industry
description: Energy systems and components frequently encountered in industrial environments; application of basic principles of thermodynamics, heat transfer, fluid mechanics and electrical machinery to the analysis and design of industrial system components and systems; improved energy utilization.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in MEEN 260 and MEEN 315 .

title: Intuitive and Counter-Intuitive Mechanisms
description: Spatial descriptions and transformations; forward and inverse kinematics of mechanical manipulators; manipulation; dexterity and manipulability; principles of manipulator-mechanism design; mobility; motion planning; control, reachability and complexity measures; workspace analysis.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Senior classification. Cross Listing: AERO 438/MEEN 407 .

title: Mechanics of Robotic Manipulators
description: Forward and inverse kinematics and differential kinematics of robot manipulators, path planning, motion planning, dynamics of robot manipulators and control algorithms; PD/PID control, computed torque algorithm.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: MEEN 364 or equivalent; junior or senior classification.

title: Internal Combustion Engines
description: Thermodynamics of cycles for internal combustion engines and gas turbines, including fuels and combustion; performance characteristics of various types of engines.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: MEEN 344 or equivalent.

title: Mechanical Controls
description: Application of classical and modern control theory techniques to modeling, analysis and synthesis of linear, mechanical control systems.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in MEEN 364 .

title: Principles of Precision Machine Design
description: Contemporary precision machine systems for manufacturing, metrology and industrial automation; integration of all aspects of mechanical engineering knowledge such as engineering mechanics, dynamics, vibration, fluid mechanics, control, sensing and measurement and heat transfer; machine and measurement tool design principles.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in MEEN 260 , MEEN 363 and MEEN 364 .

title: Smart Building Technology
description: Fundamentals of smart building technologies, design, control, integration, operation of smart buildings such as grid-interactive efficient buildings; utilization of various tools including building energy modeling (BEM) and simulation, an integrated building energy and control testbed with a small-scale building automation system; sensors and Internet of Things (IoT) devices, smart building envelope, building automation system, automated system optimization, grid-interactive efficient buildings (GEB).
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in MEEN 315 or AREN 330 . Cross Listing: AREN 416/MEEN 416 .

title: Basics of Plasma Engineering and Applications
description: Basic plasma properties and confinement techniques; single particle orbits in electric and magnetic fields, moments of Boltzmann equation and introduction to fluid theory; wave phenomena in plasmas and introduction to plasma kinetic theory; analysis of laboratory plasmas and plasma applications including fusion, electric propulsion, materials processing and plasmas enhanced chemistry.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in PHYS 207 or equivalent; senior classification in nuclear, mechanical or aerospace engineering, or physics. Cross Listing: NUEN 417/MEEN 417 .

title: Thermal-Fluids Analysis and Design
description: Integration of thermodynamics, fluid mechanics and heat transfer through application to the design of various thermal systems comprised of several components requiring individual analyses; analysis of the entire system; representative applications of thermal-fluids analysis with a design approach.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in MEEN 461 .

title: Machine Learning for Mechanical Engineers
description: Machine learning techniques with applications to the analysis and design of mechanical, fluid, thermal, material and multidisciplinary systems; linear and kernel support vector machines; neural networks; Bayesian techniques; decision trees and random forests; dimension reduction and model selection; data management and learner validation strategies; tools and application studies.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in MEEN 357 .

title: Advanced System Dynamics and Controls
description: Unified framework for modeling, analysis, synthesis, design and simulation of mechanical systems with energy exchange across multiple domains; study of mechanical, electrical, hydraulic and thermal subsystems; Newtonian mechanics, rigid body dynamics, multiple degrees of freedom vibrations and control system design.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: MEEN 364 ; junior or senior classification.

title: Automotive Engineering
description: Introduction to vehicle dynamics; application of engineering mechanics principles to analysis of acceleration and braking, cornering and handling; analysis and design of drive train, suspension, brakes, and tires to achieve desired performance.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: MEEN 363 .

title: Mechatronics
description: Basic principles of digital logic and analog circuits in mechanical systems; electrical-mechanical interfacing; sensors and actuators; digital control implementation; precision design and system integration.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: MEEN 364 or equivalent.

title: Dynamics and Modeling of Mechatronic System
description: Mechatronic interactions in lumped parameter and continuum systems; review of integral and differential electromagnetic laws, including motions; lumped elements and dynamic equations of motion; linear and nonlinear actuators and transducers; field transformation and moving media; electromagnetic force densities and stress tensors.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: MEEN 364 .

title: Compressed Air Systems
description: Basic principles, thermodynamics, and performance of compressed air systems including various components, such as compressors, reciprocating, rotary, centrifugal, and axial, prime movers, coolers, intercoolers, aftercoolers, dryers, heat recovery, receivers, separators, filters, regulators, fault detectors, controllers etc.; exploration of performance and analysis and operating principles for both systems and components, energy consumption and economic analysis for system design and operation.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in MEEN 315 or equivalent; junior or senior classification.

title: Principles of Heating, Ventilating and Air Conditioning
description: Application of thermodynamics fluid mechanics, and heat transfer to the design of HVAC equipment; selection of equipment, piping and duct layouts.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of a C or better in MEEN 344 or equivalent.

title: Principles of Building Energy Analysis
description: Analysis of building energy use by applying thermodynamics and heat transfer to building heating and cooling load calculations; heat balance and radiant time series calculation methods; psychometric analysis, indoor air quality, effect of solar radiation on heating and cooling of buildings; required design project.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: MEEN 315 or equivalent; junior or senior classification.

title: Solar Energy Engineering
description: Solar energy; solar angles and radiation; solar thermal systems; solar water heating and space heating; concentrated solar power; energy storage; solar photovoltaics; solar cell manufacturing; other solar energy technologies.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: MEEN 315 .

title: Bio-inspired Engineering Design
description: Expand design space available to engineering by developing and understanding of how nature solves problems; study of effective bio-inspired design and biomimetic applications to draw solutions from nature; enhance concept generation through the use of bio-inspired design; use current state of the art methods in bioinspired design; view nature’s solutions to different problems form an engineering perspective.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: MEEN 368 , BMEN 361 , or BAEN 375 .

title: Design of Mechanical Components and Systems
description: Design of machine elements, characteristics of prime movers, loads and power transmission elements as related to mechanical engineering design.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: MEEN 368 .

title: Computer Aided Engineering
description: Effective and efficient use of modern computer hardware and software in modeling, design, and manufacturing; simulation of a broad spectrum of mechanical engineering problems.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in MEEN 363 and MEEN 368 .

title: Finite Element Analysis in Mechanical Engineering
description: Introduction to basic theory and techniques; one- and two-dimensional formulations for solid mechanics applications; direct and general approaches; broader aspects for field problems; element equations, assembly and solution schemes; computer implementation, programming and projects; error sources and application consideration.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: MEEN 357 and 368 or equivalents.

title: Mechanics of Compliant Materials
description: Study of mechanics; three-dimensional analysis tools and techniques needed to model linear behavior of fluids and solids in response to imposed loads and deformations.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in MEEN 344 .

title: Viscoelastic Materials
description: Mechanical and mathematical basis for modeling linear viscoelastic materials which focus on polymeric solid materials; characterization of viscoelastic material properties from experimental tests; applications of stress and deformation relationships for viscoelastic structural members subjected to axial, torsional, and bending loads.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in MEEN 368 .

title: Additive and Subtractive Processes in Custom Manufacturing
description: Machining theory; traditional and non-traditional machining processes; CNC machines and tools; geometric dimensioning and tolerance (GD&T); additive manufacturing systems and processes; materials in additive manufacturing.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in MEEN 360 and MEEN 361 , or equivalent.

title: Tribology-Mechanical Interface Design
description: History and significance of tribology, rough surfaces, hertzian contact, rough surfaces in contact, friction of surfaces in contact, surface failures/wear, boundary lubrication, fluid properties, thick film lubrication, thin film lubrication, micro- and nano-tribology.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in MEEN 344 and MEEN 368 .

title: Engineering with Plastics
description: Polymer structure, processing, property characterization at the molecular, microscopic and macroscopic dimensional levels for thermosets, thermoplastics, elastomers, fibers and advanced fibrous nonparticle filled composites and smart multi-performance structures.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in MEEN 360 or MSEN 222/MEEN 222 .

title: Processing and Characterization of Polymers
description: Introduction of flow behavior in polymers; structure-property-process relationship; mixing rules for polymer blends; mechanical properties; laboratory demonstrations: injection molding, extrusion, melt mixing, and study of morphology using OM, SEM, and TEM.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in PHYS 206 , CHEM 120 , or CHEM 107 .

title: Sound and Vibration Measurements
description: Basic acoustics, review of vibration theory, wave propagation in vibrating systems, sound radiation from vibrating systems, sound and vibration sensors and instrumentation, data acquisition systems, measurement techniques, spectral analysis, spatial FFT analysis, design of experiments with vibro-acoustic systems, applications.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: MEEN 363 ; MATH 308 .

title: Corrosion Engineering
description: Basic corrosion phenomena are described, including mixed potential theory, types of corrosion, experimental methods, and prevention techniques.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: MEEN 360 and MEEN 361 , or equivalent.

title: Heat Transfer
description: Heat transfer by conduction, convection and radiation: steady and transient conduction, forced and natural convection, and blackbody and gray body radiation; multi-mode heat transfer; boiling and condensation; heat exchangers.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in MEEN 344 .

title: Cogeneration Systems
description: Design and analysis of cogeneration system; selection of the prime mover, matching power and thermal needs, institutional factors, economic evaluations, financial options and the study of actual and hypothetical systems.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: MEEN 421 or equivalent.

title: Heat Transfer Laboratory
description: Basic measurement techniques in conduction, convection, and radiation heat transfer; experimental verification of theoretical and semi-empirical results; uncertainty analysis.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in MEEN 345 ; grade of C or better or MEEN 461 or concurrent enrollment.

title: Mechanical Behavior of Materials
description: Fundamentals of flow and fracture in metals, emphasizing safe design by anticipating response of materials to complex stress and environmental service conditions; micromechanisms of flow, fatigue, creep and fracture; fracture mechanics approach to design; special emphasis given to microstructure-mechanical property relationship and damage tolerant design.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: MEEN 360 and MEEN 361 .

title: Alternative Energy Conversion
description: Design and analysis of alternative energy conversion processes and systems that are based on converting energy directly (e.g., fuel cells, photovoltaics), utilizing non-combustible heat sources (e.g., geothermal, ocean gradients, solar and nuclear fission and fusion) and obtaining energy from the environment (e.g., wind, hydroelectric, ocean tides and waves).
prerequisites: Prerequisite: MEEN 315 .

title: Elements of Composite Materials
description: Fundamentals concerned with relating structure of multiphase materials to physical properties; plastic, metallic and ceramic matrices reinforced with continuous and discontinuous fibers, whiskers and particulates.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in MEEN 360 , MEEN 361 , and MEEN 368 .

title: Gas Dynamics
description: Fundamental analysis of compressible flows and its application to supersonic airfoils/projectiles, jet and rocket nozzles, normal and oblique shock waves, explosion waves, shock tubes, supersonic wind tunnels, and compressible pipe flows.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: MEEN 344 .

title: Agile Technology Leadership
description: Exploration of a new, proven leadership system targeted at the complex and fast-paced technology development of the 21st century. Analysis of shared consciousness and common purpose to entrust and empower teams to develop new products faster. Exposure to leadership tools that provide structure and discipline to implement and sustain this innovation development system.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in ENGL 103 or ENGL 104 , and ENGR 102 ; junior or senior classification in the College of Engineering.

title: Materials in Design
description: The heuristics of synthesis of material properties, configuration and processing in the optimization of material selection in the design process; product design and development overview, failure mode effects analysis, design margin establishment; role of the generic failure modes and codes and standards; fundamental characteristics of process methods.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in MEEN 360 and MEEN 361 .

title: Air Pollution Engineering
description: Design of air pollution abatement equipment and systems to include cyclones, bag filters and scrubbers; air pollution regulations; permitting; dispersion modeling; National Ambient Air Quality Standards.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in BAEN 340 , CVEN 311/EVEN 311 , EVEN 311/CVEN 311 , or MEEN 344 . Cross Listing: BAEN 477/MEEN 477 .

title: Optical Techniques for Engineers
description: Basics of geometrical and physical optics with an emphasis on topics that often arise in multidisciplinary engineering applications such as high-speed imaging, flow visualization, particle diagnostics, chemical sensing, materials characterization, precision measurements, and a host of other applications involving cameras and lasers; related fundamentals covered in-depth, while selected set of applications discussed to outline the depth and breadth of the field.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: PHYS 206 or equivalent; senior classification.

title: Internship
description: Professional internship with a private entity, governmental agency, research institute, higher education institute, or other organizations that provide work or research experience related to mechanical engineering.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Junior or senior classification; approval of internship provider and instructor.

title: Directed Studies
description: Special problems relating to a specific project in some phase of mechanical engineering; a commitment of two semesters with 6 hours 485 credit is required.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Approval of department head and senior classification.

title: Human Sensing Technologies
description: Exploration of the cutting-edge human sensing technologies with a focus on human presence detection, counting, identification and activity tracking. Upon completion of this course, the student will have been exposed to key human sensing techniques from sensor design, working principles, signal processing, communication, firmware, software development, integration and testing.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in MEEN 260 and MEEN 357 .

title: Special Topics in...
description: Selected topics in an identified area of mechanical engineering.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Approval of instructor.

title: Research
description: Research conducted under the direction of faculty member in mechanical engineering. May be repeated 2 times for credit. Registration in multiple sections of this course is possible within a given semester provided that the per semester credit hour limit is not exceeded.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Junior or senior classification and approval of instructor.
repeatability: May be repeated 2 times for credit.

title: Laser Material Processing for Manufacturing
description: Fundamentals on laser and thermal processing of materials, laser absorption and reflection, optical and radiative property of materials, thermal and non-thermal processes, numerical simulation of lasermaterial processing, applications in semiconductor manufacturing, additive manufacturing, and micro/nanomanufacturing.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in MEEN 315 .

title: Innovation Mindset for Design and Research
description: Exploration of the key behaviors of innovators and how to increase innovativeness in one's work; exploration of additional learning orientations, including Design Thinking and Lean experimentation, and Leadership practices to increase innovation in teams.
prerequisites: Prerequisite: Junior or senior classification.

title: Computational Fluid Dynamics for Engineering and Biological Applications
description: Numerical techniques for solving engineering and biological problems; survey of relevant governing equations such as Navier-Stokes and biochemical and species transport equations, and methods to solve them; introduction of rigorous methods for analysis of the methods and their results; broad view of CFD.
prerequisites: Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in MEEN 344 and MEEN 357 .
